name: Cantabrian Route Natural Trail
description: This difficult 55.0 miles (88.5 km) road cycling route from Viveiro to Foz offers challenging climbs and stunning Galician coastal views.
region_intro: Road cycling around Viveiro offers diverse terrain, blending coastal paths with mountainous inland routes. The region features the Ría de Viveiro estuary, providing scenic views, alongside rolling hills and higher elevations. Cyclists encounter varied landscapes, from the dramatic coastline to lush green forests and river valleys. This combination of sea and mountain environments creates a range of road cycling opportunities. Best road cycling routes around Viveiro The most popular road cycling route is Cantabrian Route Natural Trail, a 55.0 miles (88.5 km) trail that takes about 4 hours 25 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and varied coastal and inland sections. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Burela – Mariña de Celeiro loop from Viveiro, a difficult 45.0 miles (72.4 km) path. This route provides extensive views of the coastline and the Ría de Viveiro estuary. Local road cyclists also love the San Cibrao Fishing Village – Mariña de Celeiro loop from Viveiro-Apeadoiro, a 36.5 miles (58.7 km) trail leading through coastal villages and along the estuary, often completed in about 2 hours 58 minutes. Road cycling around Viveiro is defined by its dramatic coastline, expansive estuaries, and challenging mountain ascents. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to difficult, long-distance rides. The routes in Viveiro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.0 stars. More than 500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Viveiro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Viveiro?
A: There are over 50 road cycling routes around Viveiro, catering to various skill levels. This includes 3 easy routes, 27 moderate routes, and 23 difficult routes, ensuring options for every cyclist.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on Viveiro's road cycling routes?
A: Viveiro offers a diverse road cycling landscape, blending dramatic coastal paths with challenging mountainous inland routes. You'll encounter significant elevation gains, picturesque estuaries like the Ría de Viveiro, rolling hills, and lush green forests. The well-paved surfaces provide a smooth ride through varied terrain.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Viveiro?
A: The region's mild climate generally makes it suitable for road cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn often provide pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds. Summer is also popular, especially for coastal rides, though it can be warmer.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Viveiro?
A: While many routes feature significant elevation, Viveiro does offer some easier options. There are 3 routes classified as easy, and 27 moderate routes that could be suitable for those with good fitness looking for less strenuous rides. For example, the 'Covas – Gate of Charles V loop' is a moderate option with less elevation.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, Viveiro is a haven for experienced road cyclists, with 23 routes classified as difficult. These routes feature significant elevation gains and longer distances. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e942763228/cantabrian-route-natural-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantabrian Route Natural Trail&lt;/a&gt; is an 88.5 km ride with 1,270m of elevation, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31448190" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ría de Viveiro – Mariña de Celeiro loop from Viveiro&lt;/a&gt; covers 83.5 km with 1,273m of ascent.

Q: What scenic viewpoints can I visit along Viveiro's road cycling routes?
A: Viveiro boasts several breathtaking viewpoints. You can enjoy magnificent views of the Viveiro estuary from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3173953" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tixoso Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or Mirador de San Roque. For an elevated perspective of the surrounding natural beauty, Monte Castelo offers panoramic vistas. Many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24583488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estaca de Bares Lighthouse – O Porto do Barqueiro loop&lt;/a&gt;, also provide spectacular coastal views.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or charming villages to explore near the cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. Viveiro's historic old town features preserved walls and gates, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3173944" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gate of Charles V&lt;/a&gt;. Nearby, you can explore charming fishing villages such as Celeiro and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2561440" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;O Porto do Barqueiro&lt;/a&gt;, known for its authentic character. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33649278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Cibrao Fishing Village – Mariña de Celeiro loop&lt;/a&gt; lead through these coastal communities.

Q: Can I see any natural wonders like waterfalls or ancient forests while cycling?
A: Yes, inland Viveiro offers lush natural spaces. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6453869" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Souto da Retorta&lt;/a&gt;, also known as the 'Eucalyptus Forest of Chavín,' is home to ancient eucalyptus trees, including 'El Abuelo,' one of Spain's tallest. The Pozo da Ferida is a beautiful waterfall with a natural pool, nestled in an extraordinary natural setting along the River Landro.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes in Viveiro?
A: Many of Viveiro's road cycling routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31695428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burela – Mariña de Celeiro loop from Viveiro&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24583488" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estaca de Bares Lighthouse – O Porto do Barqueiro loop&lt;/a&gt;, offering convenient round trips.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Viveiro?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.0 stars. Reviewers often praise the dramatic coastline, expansive estuaries, and challenging mountain ascents, highlighting the diverse and scenic cycling opportunities available.

Q: Are there any coastal road cycling routes in Viveiro?
A: Viveiro is renowned for its stunning coastline, and many road cycling routes incorporate coastal sections. You can enjoy extensive views of the coastline and the Ría de Viveiro estuary on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31695428" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Burela – Mariña de Celeiro loop from Viveiro&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a beautiful blend of sea and road.
tips: From the bridge over the Viveiro estuary you get a magnificent view of this formerly walled town with its stone buildings facing the water. The Viveiro estuary is part of the Galician high estuaries and is an important reservoir of marine life and birds.
Burela is a coastal town. Ride along the shores of the Cantabrian Sea. A great place to stop for lunch and try the local seafood.

A former whaling port, Foz is now a tourist center on the North Coast with 15 km of fine white sand beaches dotting this estuary. Its marshes are home to one of the largest colonies of seabirds in northwestern Spain

faqs.question: What makes the Viveiro - Foz Return road cycling route challenging?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as 'difficult' due to its significant length of approximately 88.5 kilometers (55 miles) and substantial elevation changes, with around 1,270 meters (4,166 feet) of both ascent and descent. While much of the surface is well-paved, the combination of distance and elevation requires a very good level of fitness and endurance.

faqs.question: Is the Viveiro - Foz Return trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: No, this trail is classified as 'difficult' and is not recommended for beginners. Its considerable length and significant elevation gain require very good fitness and experience in road cycling.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start the Viveiro - Foz Return route?
faqs.answer: The route typically starts in Viveiro. You should be able to find parking options within Viveiro town, which serves as a convenient starting point for this return journey.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the route?
faqs.answer: You can expect picturesque coastal views of the Galician coastline, passing through charming towns. Highlights include the magnificent views of Viveiro from the bridge over the Ría de Viveiro, spectacular soft white beach coves like Covas, and the rugged, rocky configuration of the coast. The turning point, Foz, offers 15 kilometers of white sand beaches and a beautiful natural bay. You'll also pass by Burela Beach and Foz Harbor.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring and autumn months when the weather is milder and more stable. Summer can also be pleasant, but be prepared for potentially higher temperatures and more tourists. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route overlaps with several other trails for significant portions. Notably, it covers about 30% of the Camiño natural do Roteiro do Cantábrico and the Mtb Camiño natural do Roteiro do Cantábrico, and approximately 27% of a Gravel route. Smaller sections also coincide with the Roteiro das Praias and Camino del Mar.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for this trail?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are generally required for cycling the Viveiro - Foz Return route itself. However, parts of the route pass through ZEC MONTE MAIOR, a protected area. While cycling on established roads is usually permitted, always respect local regulations, especially concerning wildlife and natural habitats within protected zones.

faqs.question: Is the Viveiro - Foz Return route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route, so while dogs are generally not suitable companions for the ride itself, many of the towns and beaches along the way may have their own regulations regarding dogs. If you plan to stop and explore with a dog, it's best to check local rules for specific areas.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several towns like Viveiro and Foz, you will find cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel and rest. Burela, another town along the route, also offers amenities.

faqs.question: What historical or cultural landmarks can I see?
faqs.answer: In Viveiro, you can explore the historic Gate of Charles V (Puerta de Carlos V), a national monument built in 1548, and wander through the town's narrow streets. Foz, the turnaround point, has a history as an old whaling port. The entire coastal setting offers a rich cultural backdrop of Galician life.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this road cycling trip?
faqs.answer: Given the route's length and difficulty, you should bring appropriate road cycling gear, including a well-maintained racebike, helmet, cycling apparel, and plenty of water and snacks. A repair kit, spare tube, and navigation device are also highly recommended. Sun protection is essential for coastal riding.
